problem.title: Count the Number of Complete Components

approaches.title: Bitset adjacency representation with component validation
approaches.summary: Represents each vertex's neighbors and itself as a bitset, then identifies components from matching adjacency bitsets. Revisions 1–4 progressively optimize the同

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Uses a boolean adjacency matrix and compares adjacency rows to determine whether each connected component is complete.
Replaces the boolean matrix with u64 bitsets and tracks visited vertices with a bitmask.

revisions.commitComment: escape early if nothing else in byte
revisions.rawCommitSubject: rust 2685: escape early if nothing else in byte
revisions.shortChangeSummary: Iterates through set bits using a manually maintained active-bit mask instead of scanning all vertex positions.

revisions.rawCommitSubject: rust 2685: Brian Kernighan's optimisation with trailing_zeros()
revisions.shortChangeSummary: Uses trailing_zeros and Brian Kernighan's bit-clearing technique to iterate set bits more efficiently.

approaches.title: Disjoint-set union with component edge counts
approaches.summary: Uses union-find to build connected components while maintaining each component's vertex and edge counts, then checks whether each component has n(n-1)/2 edges.

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Replaces adjacency bitsets with DSU connectivity, component sizes, and edge counts to identify complete components.
